articles.title: Strait of Hormuz closed again, Iran says, as ships attacked
articles.summary: Iran is blaming a US blockade for the closure, saying it breaches the ceasefire reached between the two countries.

articles.title: At least six killed in Kyiv as gunman opens fire and takes hostages
articles.summary: The attacker, who took hostages inside a supermarket, was killed after a shoot-out with police, officials say.

articles.title: Pope says 'tyrants' speech was not aimed at Trump
articles.summary: The pontiff says his remarks have been misinterpreted after a spat with the US president.

articles.title: Mexico's Sheinbaum denies 'diplomatic crisis' with Spain after conquest row
articles.summary: The Mexican president says there were never tension over Spainish colonisation, which had become a thorny issue.

articles.title: Madonna joins Sabrina Carpenter to surprise Coachella
articles.summary: The Queen of Pop makes a guest appearance during the Espresso singer's headline set.

articles.summary: President Emmanuel Macron said France had "loved, dreamed and grown up" with the stalwart of French cinema.

articles.title: Building of Trump's White House ballroom can resume in full, appeals court says
articles.summary: Construction at the White House is expected to continue until the next hearing, due in June.

articles.title: DR Congo accepts first set of deportees from the US
articles.summary: The Congolese government stresses those expelled from the US are only in the country temporarily.

articles.title: French peacekeeper killed in southern Lebanon
articles.summary: President Macron blames the attack on Hezbollah. The Iran-backed armed group denies "any connection" to the incident.

articles.title: HiPP recalls jarred baby food in Austria over contamination fears
articles.summary: Police are investigating whether jars sold in Spar shops were criminally interfered with.
